APPEARANCES:
Atty. ROBERT B. ABEL, JR., Esq. of Law Offices of Robert B. Abel, Jr. (Tel: 415-461-5400) for plaintiff Nickodem Ridzinski;
Atty. GREGORY J. SOWDER, Esq. of Stratman & Williams-Abrego (Tel: 510-457-3440) for defendant Jonathan Wiesler and specially appearing for defendant Rita Radvanszki (via Zoom videoconference).

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JONATHAN WIESLER's Motion To Quash Plaintiff's Service By Publication Of Defendant Rita Radvanszki.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to quash is denied.

"The only method of service under California law which does not require the transmission of documents abroad, and consequently does not implicate the Hague Service Convention, is service of summons by publication where the party's address remains unknown during the publication period despite the exercise of reasonable diligence." (Kott v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 45 Cal.App.4th 1126, 1136.)

Here, Plaintiff attempted to serve Defendant Radvanszki with reasonable diligence, which included 13 attempts at her home address, by mail, and through trying to obtain her address with internet searches. (Opp. 5:12-28.) The private investigator hired by Defendant's counsel after the publication period, stated he believes Defendant Radvanszki is likely in the Czech Republic, but that he could not locate an address for her there, and also notes that the accuracy of the information he gave is not guaranteed. (Branconier Decl., Ex. B.) Thus, Defendant Radvanszki's address was unknown during the publication period and her whereabouts are still unknown.

Order signed.
